scheme_name: PLI for Domestic Manufacturing of KSMs/APIs
overview: The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Promotion of Domestic Manufacturing of critical Key Starting Materials (KSMs)/ Drug Intermediates and Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) in the Country aims to enhance domestic manufacturing capabilities by providing financial incentives on incremental sales to eligible applicants. The scheme targets pharmaceutical manufacturers based on their Global Manufacturing Revenue (GMR) and supports the production of high-value pharmaceutical goods to reduce import dependence and strengthen India's position in the global pharmaceutical value chain. It operates over a tenure from FY 2020-21 to FY 2028-29, with incentives disbursed for 6 years based on sales performance.
objectives: Enhance India’s manufacturing capabilities by increasing investment and production in the sector
Contribute to product diversification to high value goods in the pharmaceutical sector
Create global champions out of India who have the potential to grow in size and scale using cutting edge technology
Penetrate the global value chains by enhancing domestic production capability in APIs/KSMs
Ensure higher resilience of the Indian pharmaceutical industry to external shocks
Achieve higher objective of affordable healthcare in the country and globally on a sustained basis
eligibility: Applicants shall apply within the following three groups based on the respective criteria: Group A: Applicants having Global Manufacturing Revenue (FY 2019 -20) of pharmaceutical goods and/or in vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices more than or equal to Rs. 5,000 crore. Group B: Applicants having Global Manufacturing Revenue (FY 2019 -20) of pharmaceutical goods and/or in vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices between Rs. 500 (inclusive) crore and Rs. 5,000 crore. Group C: Applicants having Global Manufacturing Revenue (FY 2019 -20) of pharmaceutical goods and/or in vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices less than Rs. 500 crore. This group shall include a sub-group for MSME applicants, i.e., applicants registered as Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) with the Ministry of MSME, Government of India.
benefits: Financial incentive on incremental sales of pharmaceutical goods based on yearly threshold criteria of minimum cumulative investment and minimum percentage growth in sales. Incentive rates: 10% for FY 2022-23 to FY 2025-26, 8% for 2026-27, and 6% for 2027-28 for Category 1 & 2 goods; 5% for FY 2022-23 to FY 2025-26, 4% for 2026-27, and 3% for 2027-28 for Category 3 goods. Additional incentive available as per para 7.2.3 of the guidelines.
financial_support: The total quantum of incentive (inclusive of administrative expenditure) under the scheme is about Rs 15,000 crore. The incentive allocation among the Target Groups is as follows: Group A: Rs 11,000 crore, Group B: Rs 2,250 crore, Group C: Rs 1,750 crore. Incentives are paid for a maximum of 6 years based on incremental sales over the base year (FY 2019-20).
application_process: 1. The applicant is required to submit the application as per application form prescribed in Appendix K. 2. The Scheme shall be open for applications during the Application Window. No application shall be accepted after the end of the Application Window. 3. An applicant can apply for one or more products as per list of eligible products as given in Appendix A. 4. An applicant shall submit an undertaking in the format given in Appendix E and also an undertaking in the format given in Appendix G consenting audit of their manufacturing site/offices for verification of information/data submitted along with the application. 5. On receipt of an application in the prescribed format, PMA will conduct an examination as per checklist in Appendix I. The aforesaid examination shall be completed within 15 working days from the date of receipt of the application or any subsequent submission of the revised application, if the original application was returned as incomplete earlier. Thereafter, the PMA shall issue an acknowledgement of receipt of the application. This acknowledgement shall not be construed as approval under the Scheme. 6. In case, on the above-mentioned examination, an application is found to be incomplete, PMA shall inform the applicant accordingly within 15 working days of receipt of the application. An applicant must complete an incomplete application within 15 days of such communication from PMA, failing which the application will be closed under intimation to the applicant. 7. A non-refundable application fee, as mentioned in Appendix C of these guidelines, would be payable for each application. The application fee would be accepted electronically only. 8. All applications will be submitted through an online portal maintained by the PMA. In case, the portal is not available, applications may be submitted in physical form to the PMA.
required_documents: Application form as per Appendix K
Undertaking in the format given in Appendix E
Undertaking in the format given in Appendix G
deadlines: The application window shall be of 60 days starting from 2nd June, 2021 to 31st July, 2021 (Both dates inclusive).
implementing_agency: Department of Pharmaceuticals, Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers, Government of India
caveats: The applicant should not have been declared as bankrupt or wilful defaulter or reported as fraud by any bank or financial institution or non-banking financial company.
Incentive is the financial benefit to be provided to each selected participant based on the incremental sales and laid down eligibility criteria.
Additional incentive is the amount that would be available to any participant in terms of para 7.2.3.
The Scheme shall be open for applications during the Application Window. No application shall be accepted after the end of the Application Window.
All non-creditable taxes and duties would be included in such expenditure.
